programmes.show_description: Dr Natalia Spierings struggles to see acne hidden with makeup by a patient. Dr Toby Nelson is shocked by a patient's DIY attempt on a cyst. Dr Adil Sheraz tackles a stubborn lipoma.
Witnessing a car crime bonanza captured by drones, CCTV, dashcams, mobile phones and police bodycams - from road raging drivers to ram raids gone wrong and high-tech car thieves.
Documentary series. The doctors deal with a woman in the midst of a panic attack, a chronic asthmatic, a man who can only eat eggs and a harrowing diagnosis of stomach cancer.
A fire alarm begins evacuation efforts; a man with potential spine injuries is given pain relief while waiting for scan results.
Dr Natalia Spierings tackles one of the biggest lipomas of her career, Dr Emma McMullen helps a bride six weeks before 'I do'; and Dr Adil Sheraz treats a patient with a face cyst.

...the Sun. New series. Dave aims to lose weight before a baby reveal, Troy plans a send-off for Nanny Tammy, and Paula hosts a bottomless brunch and tug-of-war bash to save her bar.
...the Sun. New series. Chunkz fires up a new BBQ business in Benidorm, Su and Grazie send a foster dog to the UK, and Bambi and Johnny hunt bargains for caravan staging in Spain.
In London, a detective spots a suspect stealing from a leading clothes store. In Newton Abbott, on CCTV, a chief security guard watches a man removing putty from a window. (Ep17)
Yorkshire's traffic cops are in hot pursuit of some criminals dragging a generator with just one wheel across the county, and a van full of contraband in Bradford.
A speeding murder suspect is pursued through downtown Bradford, and a speeding car thief picks a fight with a tree.
A young speeder writes his car off in a spectacular fireball, and cops pursue a car containing a familiar face...
The Operational Support Unit carry out a drugs raid at a farm, a young driver tries impersonating his brother and Craggy has an encounter with a polecat.
A criminal gang are busted in their hotel beds and Craggy and Phil arrest a serial bacon snatcher.
Observational documentary series. In this episode, PC Dodd is on a 'steak out' following a bovine break-out and Jon gets some help from a very unexpected source.
... My Car Back! Specialist car recovery companies help local governments and businesses take care of illegal or dangerous vehicles by getting them off the road.
Cash's lie catches up to him. Is Leah walking out on her marriage? Remi's symptoms multiply.
Eden's crushed by Remi's news. Mali questions Holden's motives. Cash and Levi are in the doghouse.
Emergency situations compel Dr Beecraft, Dr Glynne and Dr Swanne to refer their patients for specialist treatment.
Paramedics arrive with eight-month-old Noah, who is struggling to breathe. Kathleen, 78, who fell over, requires a CT scan and stitches for deep wounds by her eye.
The police neighbourhood teams get to grips with criminals holed up in a smart suburban semi, a firearm at a local charity and a drug dealer making a run for it. (Ep4)
When 22-year-old Ben arrives after crashing his motorbike at speed, trauma team leader Richard fears his patient may have suffered a life changing spinal cord injury.
Dr Adil Sheraz treats a bus driver with over 100 lipomas, Dr Toby Nelson tries to help a 23-year-old who's covered in keloids, plus a treat of a cyst for Dr Emma McMullen.
Detectives in the Incident Room must work tirelessly to find and bring to justice four ruthless teens who killed 63-year-old Gurmail Singh in his Huddersfield corner shop.
Dr Eleanor Beecraft fears her patient is in the middle of a drugs overdose. Dr William Laird must convince a depressed man that life is worth living.
A man named Terry, who fell off a roof and is seriously injured, needs help. A man named Jeff is brought in by ambulance after his car flipped onto its side.
There's a huge and very messy back cyst for Dr Adil Sheraz, Dr Toby Nelson treats a cheeky patient, and Dr Dev Shah meets a roofer with nine lives. (Ep5)
Challengers 'Brahms & Liszt Challenge' take on the mighty Eggheads, aiming to win the rolling prize fund.

...the Sun. New series. Andy and Becky plan their wedding in Benidorm, Ian gives his first guided tour, and Colin welcomes Margaret the pig in sunny Spain.
A woman uses the old "I was just making a call" excuse to avoid paying for goods, and a London team dealing with shoplifting and anti-social behaviour handle a disruptive gang. (Ep18)
Observational documentary series. Cops from North and West Yorkshire are cracking down on uninsured drivers whose recklessness is a cost to all of us.
The cops deal with a suicidal man with mental health problems, an Eritrean woman dumped out of a lorry by people traffickers, and a lass who's had a barney with her boyfriend.
In Leeds the cops are on the look out for a stolen Ford Fiesta; their hunt ending in a dramatic foot chase pursuing one of the mouthiest criminals they've ever seen.
A car thief careers across Mark's path, and the interceptors engage in a high-speed pursuit through Lincoln city centre.
Documentary series following the high-speed law-enforcement specialists of Lincolnshire. Jon Peacock comes across a half-naked man out on the streets in the middle of winter.
The interceptors track down a driver who has given him the slip in the side roads of Spalding, and there's a report of a dead body in Lincolnshire's waterways.

programmes.show_name: Call the Bailiffs: Time to Pay Up!
programmes.show_description: Mark and Sam trail a debtor who owes more than £20k for an unpaid invoice - but they are wary at the address as the property has an associated firearms licence and a big dog.

programmes.show_name: Fare Dodgers: At War with the Law
programmes.show_description: At Vauxhall station, one of the capital's fare-dodging ultra-hot spots, a team of six revenue protection officers set a trap to block rush-hour fare dodgers.
Shocking series that looks at tenants who are every landlords worst nightmare.
